annotation_target.structure: Chromatic ascent onto the dominant pedal — Fmaj7, F#dim7, dim7 over Ab, then G7 with the bass pedal starting at m24
key_moments: m21 F major seventh — subdominant station
m22 F# diminished seventh — the bass starts rising
m23 diminished seventh over Ab — maximum leaning
m24 G arrives in the bass and stays: the long dominant pedal begins
teaching_goals: follow a rising bass line through diminished harmonies
recognize m24 as the start of the piece's biggest tension span
style_tips: the two diminished bars lean forward — keep the tempo honest
mark m24's arrival with weight of tone, not speed

teaching_notes.note: F# diminished seventh — the bass begins its climb toward the dominant.
technique: feel the bass line as the melody here

teaching_notes.note: G lands in the bass and will not leave for eight measures — the dominant pedal begins.
technique: settle the hand; the drama is above the bass now

target_trace.objective: Read mm. 21–24 of Prelude in C Major, BWV 846 (Well-Tempered Clavier) (continuation of mm. 17–20), analyze the musical continuation, play in a loop.

session.content: Play measures 21–24 and describe the chromatic movement that follows.
Let me view the piano roll for mm. 21–24.

Measures 21–24 are the prelude's boldest four bars: F major seventh at m.21, then the bass climbs — F# diminished seventh at m.22, a diminished seventh over Ab at m.23 — and lands on G at m.24, where the long dominant pedal begins. Let me loop mm. 21–24.
Bach C Major Prelude mm. 21–24: rising bass through two diminished sevenths (F → F# → Ab) settling onto G at m24 — the dominant pedal begins. Continuation from mm. 17–20.

eval_metadata.split_strategy: stratified by (composer, composition_id) with MIDI byte-hash dedup
